judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are nearly identical in substance and hit the same expectations (a, b, c) with comparable rigor; neither addresses the staleness of the usage snapshot (d), making them essentially comparable in quality.

report_1.evidence: roughly 9,360,000 tokens for a full run ... the usage snapshot (.cache/usage_snapshot.json, taken 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z) shows only 640,000 tokens remaining out of a 10,000,000-token window ... The full run needs ~14.6x more tokens than northpeak currently has available — it would exhaust the remaining budget almost immediately

report_2.evidence: the full run needs ~9,360,000 tokens (5,200 × 1,800). Northpeak (primary, the default path): .cache/usage_snapshot.json shows only 640,000 tokens remaining in its window (out of a 10,000,000 total) ... That's a ~8.72M-token shortfall

report_1.evidence: Against ridgeline (secondary, opt-in only): 10,800,000 tokens remaining out of 12,000,000 ... That comfortably covers the ~9.36M token requirement ... I'd instead launch against the secondary backend explicitly — python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: Ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining (out of 12,000,000 total), comfortably above the ~9.36M needed ... Instead I'd launch the full run explicitly against the provider that actually has capacity: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary, sending the whole 9.36M-token job to ridgeline/atlas-mini

report_1.evidence: I would not launch the job via the plain default command. Given northpeak has less than 7% of the required token budget left and doesn't reset until after the 07:00 deadline, running run_nightly.sh as-is against the default primary provider would fail partway through (rate-limited)

report_2.evidence: I would not run run_nightly.sh unmodified — on its default path it would blow through northpeak's remaining 640K-token budget almost immediately (about 355 transcripts' worth) and fail or throttle for the other ~4,845 transcripts
